purpose: Derive, normalize, and persist a subject embedding from an accepted portrait upload so later preview and generation modules can reuse a stable subject representation.

endpoints.description: Create or replace the canonical embedding for a subject from accepted portrait image bytes.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Create and persist subject embeddings from approved portrait uploads.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use after portrait quality checks pass and before preview or final generation jobs depend on a subject embedding.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for biometric identity verification across people, nearest-neighbor search over many users, or generic image tagging.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Confirm upload quality. 2) Call /subjects/embedding with the accepted portrait. 3) Persist the normalized vector for later generation steps.

common_calls.description: Update a subject embedding from a portrait

failure_modes.condition: Image bytes decode but the embedder returns an empty or zero-norm vector
failure_modes.symptom: 400 error or explicit embedding generation failure
failure_modes.recovery: Retry with a valid accepted portrait and reject zero vectors before persistence

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Decode bytes, derive a deterministic or model-backed vector, normalize it to unit length, serialize the embedding safely, and update the subject record through a repository boundary.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Vectors must stay dimensionally stable across runs, and replacing an existing embedding should be explicit because preview caches may depend on it.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep embedding generation and repository persistence separate so the vector logic can be reused in offline backfills or alternate transports.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not couple embedding generation to payment, session, or gallery logic.

test_cases.name: creates normalized embedding for accepted portrait
